findings.remediation: Configure your test runner to generate coverage reports. For Jest: add --coverage flag. For Vitest: add coverage in config. Ensure reports are generated in the coverage/ directory.

findings.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
findings.description: No Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header configuration was found in any source file or framework config. Without CSP the application is vulnerable to cross-site scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks.
findings.remediation: Configure a Content-Security-Policy header on your server. Example: `res.setHeader('Content-Security-Policy', "default-src 'self'")`

findings.title: Missing Strict-Transport-Security header
findings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is not configured anywhere in the codebase. Without HSTS, browsers may allow insecure HTTP connections, exposing traffic to man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.remediation: Set the HSTS header with a minimum max-age of 31536000 (1 year) and include subdomains: `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload`

findings.title: Missing X-Frame-Options header
findings.description: The X-Frame-Options header is not set. The application pages can be embedded in iframes on other domains, enabling clickjacking attacks.
findings.remediation: Add `X-Frame-Options: DENY` or `X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN` to prevent framing by untrusted origins. Alternatively use CSP `frame-ancestors 'self'`.

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options header
findings.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header is not configured. Browsers may MIME-sniff responses, potentially executing malicious content as a different content type.
findings.remediation: Set `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` to prevent browsers from MIME type sniffing.

findings.title: Missing Referrer-Policy header
findings.description: No Referrer-Policy header is set. The browser may leak the full URL in the Referer header when navigating to external sites, potentially disclosing sensitive path or query parameters.
findings.remediation: Add `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin` to limit referrer information shared with third parties.

findings.title: Missing Permissions-Policy header
findings.description: The Permissions-Policy (formerly Feature-Policy) header is not present. Browser features like camera, microphone, geolocation, and payment are not explicitly restricted.
findings.remediation: Add a Permissions-Policy header to disable unused browser features: `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=()`

blueprints.summary: Found 6 security header issues across 10 checks (scanned 0 files).

findings.title: No rate limiting detected
findings.description: No rate limiting library was found in dependencies and no rate limiting code patterns were detected in source files. Without rate limiting, the application is vulnerable to brute-force attacks and denial of service.
findings.remediation: Add rate limiting to authentication endpoints and API routes. Install `express-rate-limit` or `rate-limiter-flexible` and apply it to login, registration, and password reset endpoints.

findings.title: No multi-factor authentication (MFA) support detected
findings.description: No MFA libraries (speakeasy, otplib, WebAuthn) or MFA-related code patterns (TOTP, 2FA, authenticator) were found. MFA significantly reduces the risk of account compromise from stolen credentials.
findings.remediation: Implement TOTP-based 2FA using `otplib` or `speakeasy`, or WebAuthn using `@simplewebauthn/server`. At minimum, support MFA for privileged accounts.

blueprints.summary: Found 2 authentication security issues across 8 checks (scanned 0 files).

findings.title: No structured logging library detected
findings.description: No logging library (winston, pino, bunyan, log4js) was found in project dependencies. The application likely relies on bare `console.log` calls which lack structure, levels, and context required for compliance audit trails.
findings.remediation: Install a structured logging library: `npm install pino` (fastest) or `npm install winston` (most popular). Configure JSON output format with timestamps and log levels.

findings.title: No HTTP request logging middleware detected
findings.description: No request logging middleware (morgan, pino-http, express-winston) or custom request logging patterns were found. HTTP request logs are essential for security monitoring, incident response, and compliance auditing.
findings.remediation: Add HTTP request logging middleware. For Express: `npm install morgan` and `app.use(morgan("combined"))`. For structured logs: `npm install pino-http` and `app.use(pinoHttp())`. Ensure logs include method, path, status, duration, and client IP.

findings.title: No error logging patterns detected
findings.description: No error logging calls (logger.error, console.error, error handler middleware, Sentry) were found in the codebase. Unlogged errors make it impossible to detect attacks, debug issues, or satisfy compliance requirements.
findings.remediation: Add error logging to all catch blocks and implement a global error handler middleware. Use `logger.error()` with the full error object and request context. Consider integrating an error tracking service like Sentry.

findings.title: No audit trail implementation detected
findings.description: No audit logging patterns (auditLog, audit_trail, auditEntry, activityLog) were found in the codebase. An audit trail that records who did what and when is required by SOC-2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, and ISO-27001 for accountability and incident forensics.
findings.remediation: Implement an audit logging module that records: actor (user ID), action (create/read/update/delete), resource type, resource ID, timestamp, and IP address. Store audit entries in a dedicated database table or external audit service.

blueprints.summary: Found 4 audit logging issues across 9 checks (scanned 0 files).

findings.title: No lockfile found in repository
findings.description: No package-lock.json, yarn.lock, or pnpm-lock.yaml was found. Without a lockfile, dependency versions are non-deterministic across installs, enabling supply-chain drift.
findings.remediation: Run `npm install` (or the equivalent for your package manager) to generate a lockfile and commit it to the repository.

blueprints.summary: Found 1 dependency hygiene issue across 0 production and 0 dev dependencies.

findings.title: .gitignore missing entries for sensitive files
findings.description: The .gitignore file does not include patterns for: .env, *.pem, *.key, credentials.json. Without these entries, sensitive files could accidentally be committed to the repository.
findings.remediation: Add the following entries to .gitignore: .env, *.pem, *.key, credentials.json. Review existing git history for any previously committed sensitive files.

findings.title: No compliance configuration file found
findings.description: No compliance configuration file (.certus.yml, .compliance.yml, compliance.json) was found in the repository. A compliance configuration defines which controls are active, maps them to frameworks, and documents exceptions.
findings.remediation: Create a .certus.yml or .compliance.yml file in the repository root that defines enabled compliance controls, framework mappings, and any accepted risk exceptions.

findings.title: No security or compliance policy documentation found
findings.description: No security policy documents (SECURITY.md, COMPLIANCE.md) were found in the repository. Policy documentation is a foundational requirement for SOC-2, ISO-27001, and most compliance frameworks.
findings.remediation: Create a SECURITY.md file documenting your security vulnerability disclosure policy. Add COMPLIANCE.md describing applicable frameworks, controls, and your compliance posture. Consider placing detailed policies in a docs/security/ directory.

findings.title: No CODEOWNERS file found
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS file was found in the repository (checked root, .github/, and docs/). CODEOWNERS enforces mandatory code review by designated owners for specific paths, ensuring changes to critical code are reviewed by qualified personnel.
findings.remediation: Create a CODEOWNERS file in .github/ or the repository root. Define owners for security-critical paths: `* @security-team`, `/src/auth/ @auth-team`, `/infrastructure/ @devops-team`.

findings.title: No pull request template found
findings.description: No pull request template was found in .github/pull_request_template.md. PR templates standardize the change management process by prompting developers to document changes, testing, and risk assessment.
findings.remediation: Create .github/pull_request_template.md with sections for: description, type of change, testing performed, security considerations, and compliance impact.

findings.title: No branch protection configuration evidence found
findings.description: No branch protection rules, required status checks, or review requirements were found in repository configuration files. Branch protection is essential for enforcing change management controls, requiring code review before merge.
findings.remediation: Enable branch protection on the main branch via GitHub Settings > Branches. Require at least 1 approving review, require status checks to pass, enforce for administrators. Consider managing branch protection as code with Terraform github_branch_protection resource.

findings.title: No CI/CD pipeline configuration found
findings.description: No CI/CD configuration files (.github/workflows, .gitlab-ci.yml, Jenkinsfile) were found. Without a CI pipeline, there are no automated quality or security gates in the development process.
findings.remediation: Set up a CI pipeline using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or your preferred CI system. Include at minimum: linting, testing, security scanning, and build verification.

findings.title: No CONTRIBUTING.md file found
findings.description: No contributing guidelines document was found. CONTRIBUTING.md defines the development workflow, coding standards, and review process, which are important for consistent change management.
findings.remediation: Create a CONTRIBUTING.md documenting: development setup, branching strategy, commit conventions, testing requirements, and code review process.

blueprints.summary: All 9 cloud posture checks passed. Analyzed 0 Terraform and 0 CloudFormation/CDK files.

findings.title: No input validation library detected
findings.description: No input validation library (zod, joi, yup, class-validator, ajv, io-ts) was found in dependencies, and no schema validation patterns were detected in source code. Without systematic input validation, the application is vulnerable to injection attacks, type confusion, and data corruption.
findings.remediation: Install a validation library: `npm install zod` (recommended for TypeScript). Define schemas for all API inputs, form submissions, and external data. Validate at the boundary where data enters the application.

blueprints.summary: Found 1 input validation issue across 7 checks (scanned 0 files).

findings.title: No error logging detected in codebase
findings.description: No error logging calls (logger.error, Sentry.captureException, console.error) were found. Without error logging, runtime failures go undetected, making incident response and debugging impossible.
findings.remediation: Implement error logging in all catch blocks and error handlers. Use a structured logger (pino, winston) with `.error()` level. Consider integrating an error tracking service (Sentry, Bugsnag) for alerting and triage.

findings.title: No unhandled promise rejection handler detected
findings.description: No `process.on("unhandledRejection")` or `process.on("uncaughtException")` handler was found. In Node.js, unhandled promise rejections can crash the process (default behavior in Node 15+), causing service outages without proper error logging.
findings.remediation: Add global handlers: `process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, promise) => { logger.error({ reason, promise }, 'Unhandled rejection'); })` and `process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => { logger.fatal(err); process.exit(1); })`. These are safety nets; always handle promises properly.

findings.title: No incident response documentation found
findings.description: No incident response plan, SECURITY.md, or runbook directory was found. An incident response plan documenting escalation procedures, communication protocols, and recovery steps is required by SOC-2, HIPAA, and ISO-27001.
findings.remediation: Create an INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md or .github/SECURITY.md documenting: 1) Incident classification criteria, 2) Escalation contacts and procedures, 3) Communication plan (internal and external), 4) Investigation steps, 5) Recovery procedures, 6) Post-incident review process.

findings.title: No monitoring or observability tooling detected
findings.description: No monitoring libraries (Sentry, Datadog, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, New Relic) were found in dependencies or code. Without monitoring, service degradations, errors, and security incidents cannot be detected in real-time.
findings.remediation: Integrate a monitoring solution. For error tracking: `npm install @sentry/node` and `Sentry.init({dsn: "..."})`. For APM: use Datadog (`dd-trace`) or OpenTelemetry. For metrics: use Prometheus (`prom-client`). Ensure all services report health and error metrics.

findings.title: No backup or disaster recovery configuration found
findings.description: No backup configuration, disaster recovery plan, or backup-related IaC resources were found. Without documented and tested backup procedures, data loss from accidental deletion, corruption, or ransomware may be unrecoverable.
findings.remediation: Document your backup and disaster recovery strategy including: 1) Recovery Time Objective (RTO), 2) Recovery Point Objective (RPO), 3) Automated backup schedules, 4) Restoration procedures, 5) Regular backup testing. For AWS, configure AWS Backup or RDS automated snapshots.

findings.title: No disaster recovery or business continuity plan found
findings.description: No disaster recovery or business continuity documentation was found. A DR plan documents how to restore service in the event of a major outage, data center failure, or catastrophic event.
findings.remediation: Create a DISASTER_RECOVERY.md documenting: 1) Critical systems and dependencies, 2) Recovery priorities, 3) Failover procedures, 4) Data restoration steps, 5) Communication plan, 6) Testing schedule. Conduct DR drills at least annually.

findings.title: No alerting or notification configuration detected
findings.description: No alerting tools (PagerDuty, OpsGenie, Alertmanager) or notification configurations were found. Without alerting, incidents may go unnoticed for extended periods, increasing the blast radius and recovery time.
findings.remediation: Configure alerting for critical metrics. Integrate PagerDuty or OpsGenie for on-call notification. Define alert rules for: error rate spikes, latency degradation, availability drops, and security events. Ensure 24/7 on-call coverage.

blueprints.summary: Found 5 incident response gaps across 7 checks.
